title: "booktx Markdown handling"

nav_tool: booktx

<!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. -->

<section id="markdown-handling">
<h1>Markdown handling</h1>
<p>Markdown support is implemented in <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">booktx.markdown_io</span></code>.</p>
<section id="what-gets-translated">
<h2>What gets translated</h2>
<p>booktx extracts inline prose from these contexts:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>paragraphs</p></li>
<li><p>headings</p></li>
<li><p>list items</p></li>
<li><p>blockquotes</p></li>
<li><p>table cells</p></li>
<li><p>emphasis and strong text when represented as inline content</p></li>
</ul>
<p>The extracted prose is segmented into records by <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">phrasplit</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="what-is-preserved">
<h2>What is preserved</h2>
<p>These are not translated directly:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>fenced code blocks</p></li>
<li><p>indented code blocks</p></li>
<li><p>inline code</p></li>
<li><p>link URLs</p></li>
<li><p>raw inline HTML</p></li>
<li><p>raw HTML blocks</p></li>
<li><p>YAML front matter keys</p></li>
</ul>
<p>Inline non-translatable content is hidden behind <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__TAG_NNN__</span></code> placeholders. Protected names are hidden behind <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__NAME_NNN__</span></code> placeholders.</p>
</section>
<section id="front-matter">
<h2>Front matter</h2>
<p>Leading YAML front matter is preserved. The extractor splits it from the body before Markdown parsing.</p>
<p>Example:</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>---
title: Example
<span class="gu">author: Alice</span>
<span class="gu">---</span>

<span class="gh"># Chapter One</span>

Hello world.
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The front matter block remains in the template. Body prose is extracted normally.</p>
</section>
<section id="links">
<h2>Links</h2>
<p>For Markdown links, the visible link text can be translated, but the URL is hidden and restored.</p>
<p>Source:</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Read [<span class="nt">the guide</span>](<span class="na">https://example.com</span>).
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Extractor behavior:</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Read [the guide](__TAG_001__).
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The target must preserve <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__TAG_001__</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="inline-code">
<h2>Inline code</h2>
<p>Inline code is hidden:</p>
<div class="highlight-markdown notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Run <span class="sb">`booktx validate .`</span> before building.
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Extractor behavior:</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Run __TAG_001__ before building.
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The target must preserve <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__TAG_001__</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="template-rebuild">
<h2>Template rebuild</h2>
<p>The extractor creates a Markdown template with internal <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__SPANTX_NNNN__</span></code> markers. These internal markers must never appear in translated chunks. Build replaces each marker with the translated span text.</p>
</section>
<section id="known-limitations">
<h2>Known limitations</h2>
<p>Markdown extraction relies on literal inline token content being findable in the original Markdown body. Very unusual Markdown that parses to inline content not present as a direct substring may leave a span unreplaced in the template.</p>
<p>Attributes, image alt text, and complex raw HTML are not translated in v1.</p>
</section>
</section>
